Off Calendar Sep-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL FEDER AND ATTY. CLAIRE COCHRAN OF THE LAW OFFICES OF DANIEL FEDER, TEL. NO. 1-415-391-9476 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SERENA MCCLAIN'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs - Plaintiff Serena McClain is awarded attorney's fees in the amount of $100,000 and costs of $28,919.39. "In ordinary civil actions, litigation over attorney fees often takes on a life of its own, becoming a major piece of litigation sometimes rivaling or even exceeding the amount involved on the merits. Attorney fee litigation can turn a simple civil case into two or even more cases ' The attorney's fee issue ... has like Frankenstein's monster taken on a life of its own and threatens to become a second major litigation ' [T]he attorneys' fees in this case have not simply become a case of the tail wagging the dog; the attorneys' fees have become the dog. This wasteful consumption of judicial resources and client money serves no public purpose and impairs the image of the legal profession." (Dorsey v. Superior Court (2015) 241 Cal. App. 4th 583, 599 (citations, internal quotations and some punctuation omitted)). A review of the papers on this motion, particularly the fees sought by plaintiff for this motion alone, shows that the foregoing quotation has considerable application to this motion. Plaintiff is entitled to fees per the parties' agreement, but the fees she seeks is grossly excessive to the result achieved, the simple nature of the case, the work involved in prosecuting a case of this nature and the reality that no reasonable person would agree to pay the fees sought by plaintiff for this case. Absent a statute authorizing the award of fees, a case like this would likely be handled on a contingency fee basis with the attorney's fees being at most 40% of the gross recovery. Neither any authority nor public policy requires or supports an award higher than that amount based on the record on this motion. 40% of gross recovery yields an attorney's fees award that is two-thirds of the amount received by the plaintiff. That amount here is $100,000. Awarding anything close to what plaintiff seeks sends a message that it is acceptable to over litigate simple cases governed by fees statutes, to conduct litigation activities far out of proportion to the amount at stake, and to maintain settlement positions that far exceed what the plaintiff ultimately chooses to accept. Defendants do not contest the costs sought by plaintiff. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR #6576 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents **Discovery Motion Set At 9:30**
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Claire Cochran of Law Offices of Daniel Feder telephone number 415-699-5203 for Plaintiff Serena McClain.
Attorney Michael Papuc telephone number 415-773-1755 for Plaintiff Serena McClain.
Kevin Rooney of Browne George Ross LLP telephone number 415-391-7100 for Defendants IFONLY, Traina Interactive Corp. and Trevor Traina.

Documents presented by the parties categorized as "Google Docs", "6 page privilege log", "Cheryl Docs" and "Calendar" were reviewed in camera. The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, PLAINTIFF SERENA MCCLAIN'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents is granted in part and denied in part as stated on the record. The Court issued rulings as to which documents needed to be produced subject to the protective order and which documents did not need to be produced. With respect to the "privilege log", all documents withheld on the ground of attorney client privilege and work product sought by this motion must be produced because IFONLY and Mr. Traina failed to meet their initial burden of showing that those documents are covered by the work product and attorney client privilege. If a writ is not filed by the end of the week, the documents are to be produced on Monday May 9, 2016. If a writ is filed, the Court's order is stayed pending the decision on the writ. Counsel for the Plaintiff is to prepare a form of order and present it to Mr. Rooney prior to providing it to the Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
